title: chrysanthemum bulbosum
intro: Chrysanthemum bulbosum, also known as the hardy chrysanthemum or Chrysanthemum grandiflorum, is a perennial plant native to China and Japan, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. The roots of Chrysanthemum bulbosum have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Chrysanthemum bulbosum is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and improving digestion. Chrysanthemum bulbosum is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
